channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-4535] Kernel panic - not syncing: softlockup: hung tasks
item.description:
We have a system that panics every time the MDT is under very heavy load. Below is a soft lockup, immediately followed by a panic.

We were also running ‘top’, to see what is going on. The 32 kiblnd_sd tasks are very busy, as are the 72 mdt_rdpg tasks. I have put the top output below.

We are running Lustre 2.1.5. Each MDS has 32 cores of Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2660 0 @ 2.20GHz, with 128 GB of RAM.

Since this panic occurs quite regularly, I’d really like around or a fix. Could decreasing the number of threads help? Which threads?

Thanks.

item.environment:
We are running Lustre 2.1.5.
 Each MDS has 32 cores of Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2660 0 @ 2.20GHz, with 128 GB of RAM.
item.summary: Kernel panic - not syncing: softlockup: hung tasks

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Because there is a work around, I am OK with the issue being closed.
Am I OK to go ahead and call this resolved? Thanks.

> yes I think it's reasonable to decrease this on OSS as well, we actually have less
> threads by default in 2.3 and later releases, so performance is not an issue here.

Thanks. This option seems valuable for the 2.1.5 release. Is it OK to leave in that option for a 2.3 (or 2.4 or 2.5 release), or should I remove it for 2.3, so that we get the default behavior?
yes I think it's reasonable to decrease this on OSS as well, we actually have less threads by default in 2.3 and later releases, so performance is not an issue here.

Liang,
Adding "options ko2iblnd schedulers=8" on the MDSes seems to have resolved the issue. Thank you very much!

Should this be done on the OSSes as well?
What are the performance impacts for meta data and object data?

I have failed over, and I have verified that there are only 8 kiblnd threads.
I would like to know if changing the number of mdt_rdpg or mdt threads might be helpful. Please let me know.
Thanks.

> not sure about the reason yet, but one thing may be a little helpful is
> setting "options ko2iblnd schedulers=8" which will decrease kiblnd schedulers from 32 to 8.

Thanks. I will try that. Prior to the crash, I was running
nohup top -b -d 30 > top.log 2>&1

I noticed that the number of mdt_rdpg_ threads was 12 all day long. Then, about an hour before the crash, the number of mdt_rdpg threads went up quite rapidly. Near the crash, they hit 72.

Right now (on the peer MDS that currently has the MDT mounted), they are are 23. Might it help to set the max to 23 (or some other value), by writing a 23 to /mdt_readpage/threads_max ?

Also, an hour before the crash, the number of mdt threads went up from 85 to 95. Might it help to set mdt/threads_max to 85?
not sure about the reason yet, but one thing may be a little helpful is setting "options ko2iblnd schedulers=8" which will decrease kiblnd schedulers from 32 to 8.
